message:
fix(quota,scoring): skip redundant status fetch when commands overlap, throttle score pruning to once per 24h

Two independent efficiency improvements:

quota/fetcher.go: When a provider's StatusCmd equals its UsageCmd (same
command for both), the fetcher previously ran the command twice — once
for usage, once for status. Now it detects the overlap:
- If StatusCmd == UsageCmd, parse the already-captured output for status
  data too (zero extra PTY round-trips)
- If StatusCmd is empty, skip the status fetch entirely
- Otherwise fetch status separately as before
This halves PTY interaction time for providers like Claude Code where
usage and status are reported by the same command.

scoring/tracker.go: Record() previously called pruneLocked() on every
single score write, scanning and truncating the JSONL file on every
invocation. With agents recording dozens of scores per minute, this
caused unnecessary I/O pressure. Now tracks lastPrune timestamp and
only prunes when 24 hours have elapsed since the last prune, matching
the retentionDays granularity. Adds a lastPrune field to the Tracker
struct to persist the throttle state across calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
